note: Bounded local agents, teams, mailboxes, progress, cancellation, and lifecycle replace source decomposition units.
Cancellation, queues, timeouts, shutdown, and concurrent-turn state are explicit and bounded.
Slash discovery, argument completion, prompt submission, and interactive command state use one provider-neutral path.
Trusted user configuration is typed while project settings remain deny-only and cannot inject environment authority.
Instruction discovery, token bounds, and transactional compaction preserve the generic context lifecycle.

Exact estimated tokens are partitioned across instructions, tools, messages, results, media, and thinking; terminal and control views expose capacity, memory status, top tools, and bounded actionable warnings.
Hooks, plugins, skills, output styles, and custom commands share trusted bounded extension boundaries.
Workspace-confined discovery, reads, freshness checks, atomic writes, glob, and grep cover filesystem helpers.
Repository discovery, checkpoints, diffs, rewind, and owned worktree lifecycle are explicit and fail closed.
Private bounded cross-session prompt history supports cycling, scoped reverse search, preview, accept, and execute.
Plain transcript export and bounded clipboard rendering preserve the user-facing export purpose without proprietary image or recording formats.
Local debug, doctor, status, and status-line diagnostics replace telemetry-backed or runtime-specific observability helpers.
Configured MCP and LSP transports, discovery, authorization, synchronization, and bounds are implemented natively.
Images, clipboard paste, PDF extraction, and notebook edits are normalized and bounded before model use.
Opt-in private workspace memory supports extraction, indexing, recall, consolidation, and stale-state rejection.
Provider-neutral Messages, Chat Completions, and Responses adapters share strict streaming and message invariants.
Explicit absolute-path CA and mTLS PEM files are bounded, no-follow validated, parsed before networking, scrubbed from child environments, and applied to HTTP and WebSocket clients.
Permission-before-execution, explicit elevation, path confinement, and network sandboxing preserve authority boundaries.
Cross-platform process groups or job objects, bounded output, shell parsing, and sandbox dispatch replace process helpers.
Private modes, no-follow validation, atomic replacement, hashes, and authenticated local state replace generic storage helpers.
Private sessions support clear, resume, branch, tag, title, preview, transcript, and bounded recovery.
Bash, zsh, and fish scripts are generated from the live Clap command; stdout is default and explicit file output is private, create-only, and symlink-safe.
Sanitized status, notices, idle completion notifications, and progress rendering are local and configurable.
Persistent work items, background tasks, workflows, cron, wakeups, and task UI have bounded lifecycle state.
Original Rust inline/fullscreen rendering, selection, markdown, Vim, keybindings, resize, and modal coordination cover terminal primitives.
Strict schemas, deferred discovery, ordered tool results, bounded persistence, and exactly-once structured output cover tool helpers.

Quick Open and cancellable Global Search provide bounded fuzzy/file-text results, responsive previews, source-style open/mention/path actions, PTY fallbacks, read-deny enforcement, and no-follow workspace confinement.
Small collection, memoization, grouping, and resolver utilities are consolidated into typed Rust ownership and standard collections.
JSON, YAML-adjacent configuration, schema conversion, IDs, and semantic parsing are consolidated into serde and strict validators.
Width-aware truncation, highlighting, formatting, words, and ANSI slicing are consolidated in the terminal and markdown layers.
OS paths, executable discovery, terminal setup, XDG directories, and platform branching are implemented through Rust platform APIs.
The stateful Rust terminal coordinator replaces React application-shell composition.
Typed terminal dialogs, fuzzy selectors, confirmation, tabs, and modal suspension replace reusable dialog components.
Streaming messages, tool summaries, transcript expansion, markdown, compaction markers, and selection share one bounded renderer.
Typed rejection, validation, interruption, and fallback messages are rendered at their Rust error boundary.
Checkpoint summaries and structured permission previews replace source diff component decomposition.
Agent progress, task lists, workflow selection, teammate state, and background controls share one task dialog model.
Model, theme, output style, settings, trust, and keybinding views use typed Rust selectors and validation.
Reusable rows, boxes, dividers, loading states, selectors, and shortcuts are represented by compact terminal frame primitives.
Effect cleanup, timers, polling, cancellation, and first-render behavior are explicit Rust state-machine transitions.
Input, paste, history, search, typeahead, keybinding, selection, and Vim hooks are integrated into the editor state machine.
Merged catalogs and watched settings, skills, tools, MCP clients, and task state use generation-checked Rust snapshots.
Hosted pull-request, issue, repository mapping, and forge-account polling are vendor-specific and outside a provider-neutral local harness.
Subscription, OAuth account, API-key onboarding, quota, billing, and entitlement surfaces are outside scope.
Branded hints, attribution, onboarding, bundled modes, product copy, and identity assets are not migrated.
Desktop handoff, browser-extension bridges, custom URL schemes, and desktop import paths exceed the local harness boundary.
Vendor IDE RPC, auto-connect, selection, and marketplace integration are excluded; generic LSP remains implemented separately.
Hosted remote sessions, ingress, SSH relay, teleport, bridge, and account-backed history are outside local scope.
Analytics, experiments, fingerprints, event sinks, privacy controls, and first-party workload tags are intentionally absent.
Vendor distribution channels, installers, package-manager mutation, release feed, and self-update UI are outside runtime scope.
Hosted organization policy, managed environment injection, and model classifiers cannot elevate local authority.
Provider-specific betas, entitled fast modes, advisor routing, and branded model policy are not harness primitives.
Official marketplace recommendations and hosted plugin promotion are excluded; explicit trusted local plugins remain implemented.
Microphone capture, speech services, keyterms, and voice interaction are outside scope.
Desktop capture and input injection exceed the coding-harness authority boundary.
Employee-only recording, heap, profiler, fault injection, and runtime performance instrumentation are not product behavior.
Build-gated proactive delivery, away cards, and hosted idle-return experiments are not active generic CLI behavior.
First-party experiment routing an entire conversation to a second proprietary reviewer model is outside scope.
Vendor feedback upload and survey collection require telemetry and hosted identity channels.
Brand logos, stickers, icons, and purely decorative product assets are not copied.
Provider-specific cloud credential status and SDK authentication are outside the configurable HTTP protocol core.
